description: Replace the existing CDK infrastructure stack with a full serverless stack including: DynamoDB table with encryption, Lambda functions, API Gateway with Cognito authorizer, S3 bucket with CloudFront distribution for frontend hosting, Cognito user pool for authentication, and KMS encryption where applicable.
steps: Replace apps/infra/src/stacks/recipe-manager-stack.ts with a new comprehensive stack that includes: (1) KMS key for encryption. (2) DynamoDB table with partition key 'userId' (String) and sort key 'id' (String), with encryption using KMS key, point-in-time recovery enabled, removal policy DESTROY for dev. Add a GSI 'byCategory' with partition key 'userId' and sort key 'createdAt' for listing recipes sorted by date. (3) Cognito UserPool with self-signup enabled, email as sign-in alias, password policy (min 8 chars, require lowercase, uppercase, digits, symbols), account recovery via email. UserPoolClient with auth flows: USER_PASSWORD_AUTH, USER_SRP_AUTH. (4) S3 bucket for frontend hosting with KMS encryption, block all public access, removal policy DESTROY with auto-delete objects. (5) CloudFront distribution using S3 as origin with OAC (Origin Access Control), default root object index.html, viewer protocol policy redirect-to-https, custom error responses for SPA routing (403->index.html, 404->index.html). (6) NodejsFunction for the API Lambda using apps/api/src/handler.ts as entry, runtime NODEJS_20_X, environment TABLE_NAME pointing to the DynamoDB table, grant read/write on the table to the Lambda. (7) RestApi with Cognito authorizer on all methods except OPTIONS, CORS configured for all origins, proxy integration with the Lambda. (8) CfnOutputs for: API URL, CloudFront domain, User Pool ID, User Pool Client ID, S3 bucket name.
Update apps/infra/src/main.ts if needed (should still create the RecipeManagerStack).
Ensure apps/infra/project.json has implicitDependencies on 'api' and 'shared'.

Add any missing CDK construct library imports. The existing aws-cdk-lib and constructs dependencies should cover all needed constructs (DynamoDB, Lambda, API Gateway, S3, CloudFront, Cognito, KMS are all in aws-cdk-lib).
acceptance_criteria: Running pnpm cdk synth produces valid CloudFormation template with DynamoDB table, Lambda, API Gateway, S3, CloudFront, Cognito, and KMS resources
S3 bucket uses KMS encryption and blocks public access
CloudFront distribution uses OAC for S3 origin access
API Gateway has Cognito authorizer configured
Lambda has TABLE_NAME environment variable and DynamoDB read/write permissions
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/recipe-manager && pnpm exec tsc --project apps/infra/tsconfig.app.json --noEmit
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/recipe-manager && pnpm cdk synth 2>&1 | head -50 (verify it produces CloudFormation output)
